user_query:
I'm preparing to revise my Agile workshop and coaching materials. Please audit the attached participant feedback and two teams' retrospective notes, then produce a prioritized critique of improvement themes with evidence.

Inputs (use only these files):
- input/feedback.csv — attendee feedback with ratings and free-text comments.
- input/web_feedback.html — exported web comments embedded in HTML list items with IDs and data-rating.
- input/retrospective.md — sprint retrospective notes with explicit IDs and sections.
- input/themes.yaml — canonical theme definitions with keyword lists.

What I need:
1) Extract structured mention records from all three content sources into output/extracted_mentions.jsonl with one JSON object per mention, fields:
   - id: a unique mention ID you construct (e.g., "csv-<attendee_id>", "html-<li id>", or "md-<retroid>")
   - source_file: one of feedback.csv | web_feedback.html | retrospective.md
   - source_id: attendee_id (CSV), li/@id (HTML), or [id: ...] from the markdown bullet
   - text: the original comment/bullet text
   - sentiment: negative | neutral | positive (rules below)
   - themes: array of canonical theme names found in this mention (from themes.yaml)
   - rating: numeric rating if present (CSV/HTML), else null for markdown

   Classification rules (deterministic):
   - Theme detection: case-insensitive match if any theme keyword in input/themes.yaml appears as a whole word or phrase in the text. A single mention may count toward multiple themes.
   - Sentiment:
     • feedback.csv and web_feedback.html: rating <= 2 = negative; rating = 3 = neutral; rating >= 4 = positive.
     • retrospective.md: items under "What could be improved" = negative; items under "What went well" = positive; any other sections = neutral.

2) Produce a ranked themes table at output/themes_ranked.csv with columns:
   - theme
   - total_mentions
   - negative_mentions
   - neutral_mentions
   - positive_mentions
   - priority_score (compute as 2*negative_mentions + 1*neutral_mentions - 1*positive_mentions)
   - avg_rating_from_feedback (mean of ratings from mentions originating in feedback.csv or web_feedback.html that reference this theme; exclude markdown-only mentions; if no ratings for a theme, leave blank/NA)
   - evidence_sources (pipe-separated set from {csv, html, md} indicating which sources contributed mentions for this theme)

   Processing requirements:
   - Filter out themes with total_mentions < 2 before ranking and reporting.
   - Sort by priority_score descending; tie-break by total_mentions descending, then theme name ascending.

3) Compose a concise status-oriented critique report at output/summary.md with:
   - A brief opening line: "Analyzed N mentions across M themes from 3 sources." (N = total JSONL records; M = unique themes identified before the < 2 filter.)
   - Top 5 Improvement Themes section: list up to 5 themes in the same order as output/themes_ranked.csv with priority_score > 0. For each, include counts (neg/neu/pos), priority_score, and one representative negative quote (exact text) as a blockquote followed by a citation in parentheses indicating the source file and source_id (e.g., "feedback.csv attendee_id=7").
   - Positive Highlights section: list up to 3 themes with the highest positive_mentions (ties alphabetically), with their positive_mentions counts.
   - Next Iteration Actions section: at least 3 concrete bullets referencing specific top themes by name.

Notes/assumptions:
- Treat keyword matches case-insensitively and match whole words/phrases (e.g., "story points" should match the two-word phrase).
- A mention that matches multiple themes contributes to each matched theme's counts.
- Ensure all three outputs are created exactly at the specified paths under ./output.
- Do not access the internet.

Deliverables:
- output/extracted_mentions.jsonl
- output/themes_ranked.csv
- output/summary.md All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
